---
title: "LSF Task 1"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales) 
library(patchwork)
#library(countrycode)
#library(rnaturalearth)
library(sf)
```

```{r}
#Original Data
data1 <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\dilan\\Downloads\\Land_Use_cleaned.csv")
glimpse(data1)

#data
data2 <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\dilan\\Downloads\\Macro_Indicators_cleaned.csv")
data3 <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\dilan\\Downloads\\Value_Shares_by_Industry_and_Primary_Factors_cleaned.csv")
```

```{r}
#Manipluated Data 1
forest_data <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Forest land")

agricultural_data <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area")
```

```{r}
#Forest Data Plot
# Plot each country in its own panel
#ggplot(forest_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
  #geom_line(color = "forestgreen", size = 1) +
  #facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  #labs(
    #title = "Forest Area Trends by Country",
    #x = "Year",
    #y = "Forest Area (1000 ha)"
 # ) +
  #theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  #theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8))
```

```{r}
# Anguilla doesn't have agricultural land
# country_name <- "Anguilla"
# 
# # Filter for that country's agricultural land data
# agri_country <- data1 %>%
#   filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area", Country == country_name)
# 
# # Plot
# ggplot(agri_country, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
#   geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Agricultural Land Area in", country_name),
#     x = "Year",
#     y = "Area (1000 ha)"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r}
# country_name <- "Antigua and Barbuda"
# 
# agri_country <- data1 %>%
#   filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area", Country == country_name)
# 
# ggplot(agri_country, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
#   geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Agricultural Land Area in", country_name),
#     x = "Year",
#     y = "Area (1000 ha)"
#   ) +
#   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(agri_country$Year), max(agri_country$Year), by = 5)) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Filter agricultural land data
agri_data <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area")

# Get valid countries (excluding Anguilla by default since it has no data)
valid_countries <- sort(unique(agri_data$Country))

# Loop through and plot each country
for (country in valid_countries) {
  country_data <- agri_data %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  # Skip if empty (safety check)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Agricultural Land Area in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```
```{r}
# Filter for Value US$, 2015 prices
macro_value <- data2 %>%
  filter(Element == "Value US$, 2015 prices")

# Get sorted list of countries
macro_countries <- sort(unique(macro_value$Country))

# Loop through countries and plot
for (country in macro_countries) {
  country_data <- macro_value %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Value US$, 2015 prices in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Million USD (2015 prices)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +  # ✅ No conflict now
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)
}
```
```{r, fig.width=12}
# Filter for the US$ ratio only
ratio_usd <- data2 %>%
  filter(Element == "Ratio of Value Added (Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing) US$")

# Get sorted list of countries with data
ratio_countries <- sort(unique(ratio_usd$Country))

# Loop through countries and plot
for (country in ratio_countries) {
  country_data <- ratio_usd %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "forestgreen", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Ratio of Value Added (Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing) to the Economy - US$ in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Share of Economy (%)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +  # Convert to percentage
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
# Filter for Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing + Value US$, 2015 prices
factors_value <- data3 %>%
  filter(Industry == "Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing",
         Element == "Value US$, 2015 prices")

# Get sorted list of countries
factor_countries <- sort(unique(factors_value$Country))

# Loop through countries and plot
for (country in factor_countries) {
  country_data <- factors_value %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value, color = Factor)) +
    geom_line(size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Value US$, 2015 prices - Agriculture, Forestry & Fishing in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Million USD (2015 prices)",
      color = "Factor"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +  # No scientific notation
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
# Filter for Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing + Primary Factors Total + Value US$, 2015 prices
primary_factors_value <- data3 %>%
  filter(Industry == "Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing",
         Factor == "Primary Factors Total",
         Element == "Value US$, 2015 prices")

# Get sorted list of countries
primary_factor_countries <- sort(unique(primary_factors_value$Country))

# Loop through countries and plot
for (country in primary_factor_countries) {
  country_data <- primary_factors_value %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "darkorange", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Primary Factors Total - Agriculture, Forestry & Fishing in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Million USD (2015 prices)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +  # formatted with commas
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
# Filter for Agricultural Land
agri_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area")

# Filter for Macro Indicator: Value US$, 2015 prices
macro_value <- data2 %>%
  filter(Element == "Value US$, 2015 prices")

# Get countries in BOTH datasets
common_countries <- intersect(unique(agri_land$Country), unique(macro_value$Country))
common_countries <- sort(common_countries)

# Loop through each country and plot
for (country in common_countries) {
  
  land_plot <- agri_land %>%
    filter(Country == country) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(title = paste(country, "- Agricultural Land"),
         x = "Year",
         y = "1000 ha") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(agri_land$Year), max(agri_land$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  macro_plot <- macro_value %>%
    filter(Country == country) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1.2) +
    labs(title = paste(country, "- Value US$, 2015 prices"),
         x = "Year",
         y = "Million USD (2015 prices)") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(macro_value$Year), max(macro_value$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  # Combine side-by-side
  combined_plot <- land_plot + macro_plot
  print(combined_plot)
}
```
```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# --- filters ---
agri_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Agricultural land", Element == "Area")

macro_value <- data2 %>%
  filter(Element == "Value US$, 2015 prices")

ratio_usd <- data2 %>%
  filter(Element == "Ratio of Value Added (Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing) US$")   # (code 6163)

# Intersect countries that have all three datasets
common_countries <- Reduce(intersect, list(unique(agri_land$Country),
                                           unique(macro_value$Country),
                                           unique(ratio_usd$Country))) |> sort()

# Loop for three plots per country
for (country in common_countries) {

  land_df <- agri_land %>% filter(Country == country)
  macro_df <- macro_value %>% filter(Country == country)
  ratio_df <- ratio_usd %>% filter(Country == country)

  p_land <- ggplot(land_df, aes(Year, Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", linewidth = 1.1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(land_df$Year), max(land_df$Year), by = 5)) +
    labs(title = paste(country, "— Agricultural Land"),
         x = "Year", y = "Area (1000 ha)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  p_macro <- ggplot(macro_df, aes(Year, Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(macro_df$Year), max(macro_df$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
    labs(title = paste(country, "— Value US$, 2015 prices"),
         x = "Year", y = "Million USD (2015)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  p_ratio <- ggplot(ratio_df, aes(Year, Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "forestgreen", linewidth = 1.1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(ratio_df$Year), max(ratio_df$Year), by = 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste(country, "— Ratio of Value Added (Ag/For/Fish), US$"),
         x = "Year", y = "Share of Economy (%)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  print(p_land | p_macro | p_ratio)
}
```

```{r}
#Cropland area under conventional tillage
conventional_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Cropland area under conventional tillage")

# Loop through and plot each country
for (country in valid_countries) {
  country_data <- conventional_land %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  # Skip if empty (safety check)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Conservational Tillage -", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
#Cropland area under conservation tillage
conservation_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Cropland area under conservation tillage")

# Loop through and plot each country
for (country in valid_countries) {
  country_data <- conservation_land %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  # Skip if empty (safety check)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Conservation Tillage -", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
#Cropland area under zero or no tillage
no_tillage <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Cropland area under zero or no tillage")

# Loop through and plot each country
for (country in valid_countries) {
  country_data <- no_tillage %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  # Skip if empty (safety check)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("No Tillage -", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
#Cropland area under protective cover
protective_cover <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Cropland area under protective cover")

# Loop through and plot each country
for (country in valid_countries) {
  country_data <- protective_cover %>%
    filter(Country == country)
  
  # Skip if empty (safety check)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "sienna", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Protective Cover -", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
#slopes <- agri_data %>%
  #group_by(Country) %>%
  #do({
   # model <- lm(Value ~ Year, data = .)
    #slope <- coef(model)[["Year"]]
    #data.frame(Slope = slope)
  #})

#slopes <- slopes %>%
  #mutate(Trend = case_when(
    #Slope > 0 ~ "Increasing",
    #Slope < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
    #TRUE ~ "Stable"
  #))

#ggplot(data_with_trend %>% distinct(Country, .keep_all = TRUE),
 #      aes(map_id = Country, fill = Trend)) +
  #geom_map(map = world_map) +
  #expand_limits(x = world_map$long, y = world_map$lat) +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("Increasing" = "blue", "Decreasing" = "red", "Stable" = "grey"))
```
```{r}
library(countrycode)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(sf)
library(janitor)

slopes <- agri_data %>%
  filter(Region %in% c("SouthAmerica", "CentralNorthAmerica", "Caribbean")) %>%
  group_by(Country, Region) %>%
  do({
    model <- lm(Value ~ Year, data = .)
    slope <- coef(model)[["Year"]]
    data.frame(Slope = slope)
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Trend = case_when(
    Slope > 0 ~ "Increasing",
    Slope < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
    TRUE ~ "Stable"
  ))

world_sf <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# keep only Americas
americas_sf <- world_sf %>%
  filter(subregion %in% c("South America", "Central America", "Caribbean", "North America"))

slopes <- slopes %>% 
  mutate(Country_clean = tolower(Country))

americas_sf <- americas_sf %>% 
  mutate(Country_clean = tolower(admin))

# join trend data to map
map_data <- americas_sf %>%
  left_join(slopes, by = "Country_clean")

ggplot(map_data) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Trend)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Increasing" = "blue", "Decreasing" = "red", "Stable" = "grey")) +
  labs(title = "Agricultural Land Trends in the Americas (1961–2023)",
       fill = "Trend")

final_tableau <- slopes %>% 
  filter(Region %in% c("SouthAmerica", "CentralNorthAmerica", "Caribbean")) %>%
  select(Country, Slope, Trend, Region)
write.csv(final_tableau, "agri_trends.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
forest_data <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Naturally regenerating forest", Element == "Area")

forest_countries <- sort(unique(forest_data$Country))

for (country in forest_countries) {
  country_data <- forest_data %>% filter(Country == country)
  if (nrow(country_data) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "seagreen4", linewidth = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Naturally Regenerating Forest in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
forest_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Forest land", Element == "Area")

forest_countrys <- sort(unique(forest_land$Country))

for (country in forest_countrys) {
  country_data <- forest_land %>% filter(Country == country)
  if (nrow(country_data) < 2) next  # skip if not enough points

  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Forest Land in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```
```{r}
planted_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Planted Forest", Element == "Area")

planted_countrys <- sort(unique(planted_land$Country))

for (country in planted_countrys) {
  country_data <- planted_land %>% filter(Country == country)
  if (nrow(country_data) < 2) next  # skip if not enough points

  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Planted Forest Land in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```
```{r}
primary_land <- data1 %>%
  filter(Item == "Primary Forest", Element == "Area")

primary_countrys <- sort(unique(primary_land$Country))

for (country in primary_countrys) {
  country_data <- primary_land %>% filter(Country == country)
  if (nrow(country_data) < 2) next  # skip if not enough points

  p <- ggplot(country_data, aes(x = Year, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Primary Forest Land in", country),
      x = "Year",
      y = "Area (1000 ha)"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(country_data$Year), max(country_data$Year), by = 5)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  print(p)
}
```


```{r}
library(countrycode)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(sf)
library(janitor)

forest_slopes <- forest_land %>%
  filter(Region %in% c("SouthAmerica", "CentralNorthAmerica", "Caribbean")) %>%
  group_by(Country, Region) %>%
  do({
    model <- lm(Value ~ Year, data = .)
    slope <- coef(model)[["Year"]]
    data.frame(Slope = slope)
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Trend = case_when(
    Slope > 0 ~ "Increasing",
    Slope < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
    TRUE ~ "Stable"
  ))

world_sf <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# keep only Americas
americas_sf <- world_sf %>%
  filter(subregion %in% c("South America", "Central America", "Caribbean", "North America"))

forest_slopes <- forest_slopes %>% 
  mutate(Country_clean = tolower(Country))

americas_sf <- americas_sf %>% 
  mutate(Country_clean = tolower(admin))

# join trend data to map
map_data <- americas_sf %>%
  left_join(slopes, by = "Country_clean")

ggplot(map_data) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Trend)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Increasing" = "blue", "Decreasing" = "red", "Stable" = "grey")) +
  labs(title = "Forest Land Trends in the Americas (1961–2023)",
       fill = "Trend")

final_tableau <- forest_slopes %>% 
  filter(Region %in% c("SouthAmerica", "CentralNorthAmerica", "Caribbean")) %>%
  select(Country, Slope, Trend, Region)
write.csv(final_tableau, "forest_trends.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
